name: libheap-perl
title: libheap-perl
summary: Perl extensions for keeping data partially sorted
description:
Perl extensions for keeping data partially sorted
The Heap collection of modules provide routines that manage
a heap of elements.  A heap is a partially sorted structure
that is always able to easily extract the smallest of the
elements in the structure (or the largest if a reversed compare
routine is provided).
.
If the collection of elements is changing dynamically, the
heap has less overhead than keeping the collection fully
sorted.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libheap-perl because Perl extensions for keeping data partially sorted.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libheap-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libheap-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libheap-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
